statusLine: EU fuel reserves remain under strain with 20 of 27 countries experiencing critical shortages in at least one fuel type, despite overall reserves staying above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows mixed signals in March 2026 data. While EU-wide averages for petrol (112.8 days), diesel (101.5 days), and jet fuel (95.1 days) all exceed the mandatory 90-day minimum, the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities across member states. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, including diesel reserves at just 30.2 days.

A total of 20 of 27 EU countries show critical shortages in at least one fuel category. Jet fuel presents the greatest concern, with 16 countries below safe levels and 12 in critical status. Diesel shortages affect 11 countries critically, while petrol reserves are critical in 7 nations. Poland and Luxembourg join Romania in having all three fuel types at critical levels, indicating potential supply chain stress in Eastern and smaller European markets.

The fuel price landscape reflects ongoing market tensions, with Brent crude at $105.76 per barrel—a 45.9% increase since late February 2026. Consumer prices vary dramatically across the bloc, from Malta's €1.34 per liter for petrol to the Netherlands' €2.34, suggesting different national approaches to taxation and market intervention during this period of elevated oil costs.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel shortages in at least one category, despite EU-wide averages exceeding 90-day minimums
Romania shows the most severe stress with all fuels critical, including diesel at just 30.2 days—one-third of the mandatory minimum
Jet fuel faces the broadest shortages with 16 of 27 countries below safe levels and 12 in critical status
Brent crude has surged 45.9% since late February to $105.76/barrel, driving consumer petrol prices from €1.34 in Malta to €2.34 in Netherlands
Eastern European countries (Romania, Poland) and smaller markets (Luxembourg) show particular vulnerability with multiple critical fuel categories
